response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial and GIS experience through GISCollective and Green Map, and has touched distributed/high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby, but the role's core requirement is production-grade C++ or Rust for a high-performance routing tiling pipeline — an area where the candidate is fundamentally misaligned. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, his C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month role in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis, and he has no experience with routing data pipelines, navigation tile generation, or commercial-scale geospatial processing.
strengths: Hands-on GIS/geospatial background via GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform) and Green Map, including a FOSS4G conference talk
Experience building low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, caching)
Broad systems breadth across embedded, Linux, mobile, and web — demonstrates willingness to work across the stack
concerns: No meaningful C++ or Rust production experience; primary languages are TypeScript, JavaScript, and DLang — the role's core technical requirement is unmet
Geospatial work is NGO/hobbyist scale, not commercial navigation or routing-tile pipeline infrastructure at global traffic volumes
No experience with routing tile generation, navigation data pipelines, map data transformation, or real-time traffic integration
